[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Oct 21, 2007 5:10 pm No wonder I didn't get it. I briefly heard of that 3-parter episode but didn't see any of it yet. And from a clip, I spy with my little eye, Totoro and Astroboy. :mrgreen:

[]: Post by V » Sun Oct 21, 2007 5:45 pm yes....yes...that would be a distinct possibility.....

[]: Post by NAveryW » Sun Oct 21, 2007 6:04 pm Oh come on, how could anyone who watches South Park not have seen it yet?! It's the best episode this side of "Lice Capades"! Anyway, yeah... "Imaginationland" is the first part of the "Kyle Sucks Cartman's Balls" trilogy, all three of which will probably deal with the attacks in Imaginationland. The barrier separating the evil side from the good side was broken at the end of the first episode of the trilogy, and we can expect an epic fight between good and evil imaginary characters throughout the next two episodes. EDIT: Also, outside of "Imaginationland", they've already spoofed several anime. The ones that I can remember are Pokémon ("Chinpokomon"), Akira ("Trapper Keeper"), Ghost in the Shell ("AWESOM-O"), and fighting anime in general ("Good Times With Weapons").

[]: Post by Tokpile Quohog » Mon Oct 22, 2007 12:55 am Link's also in there too.

[]: Post by NAveryW » Mon Oct 22, 2007 3:35 pm Ehh, I wouldn't consider him to count anime-wise. There are many video game characters, including Mario, Link, Pac-Man, the Prince from the Katamari games, etc.

[]: Post by NAveryW » Wed Oct 24, 2007 8:13 pm Wooo, less than an hour to go!

[]: Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Thu Oct 25, 2007 1:04 am I. LOVE. GHOST IN. THE. SH. ELL. A. LOT. No really. Ignore the nudity, and you got yourself a pretty sik anime. It's very Evangelion, in its dealing with human issues, especially during the sequel (defining what exactly is human when robots are accurately programmed to express human emotion). WHO LIKES TOTORO?! http://www.math.sc.edu/~griggs/Family/totoro.jpg my first anime. ever! was totoro. after that i watched some astro boy, but 1st belongs to totoro. I WILL get one of those big fluffy ORIGINAL totoro plushies before I die, WHO'S WITH ME?! but returning to topic, i guess the SP animators left out Evangelion. I don't see Shinji ejaculating fireballs of doom (evil side of Imaginationland) XP

[]: Post by BrikHaus » Thu Oct 25, 2007 9:09 am After they said they had been working on a portal to another dimension, I kept thinking, "Please make a Stargate joke, please make a Stargate joke."* And a few minutes later, whaddaya know... 8) * I am a huge Stargate (SG-1) fan.

[]: Post by chee » Thu Oct 25, 2007 10:42 am TheAyanamiOtaku wrote: I. LOVE. GHOST IN. THE. SH. ELL. A. LOT. No really. Ignore the nudity, and you got yourself a pretty sik anime. It's very Evangelion, in its dealing with human issues, especially during the sequel (defining what exactly is human when robots are accurately programmed to express human emotion).

[]: Post by Themaninblack » Mon Oct 29, 2007 2:42 pm ...I'm still hoping Shinji pops up and ends up being even more evil or perverse than the Christmas critters

[]: Post by BrikHaus » Thu Nov 01, 2007 12:32 am I've got a riddle for you. What else goes on a sundae other than hot fudge and whipped cream?
